title: Seeded Recursive Backtracking Generation
description: Generation chooses visited neighbors, recursive depth overflows silently, random state is global, and completion is confused with connectivity. Learners cumulatively produce seeded iterative and recursive backtracker through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Maintain a current cell, visited set, frontier stack or call path, reciprocal passages, and a progress invariant.
Enumerate in-bounds unvisited neighbors before randomized selection and retain deterministic ordering before shuffling.
Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state.
